content.full_text:
FPPC
APR 20 4:10 PM '90

April 24, 1990

Ms. Katherine Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Regarding Use of Surplus
Campaign Funds

Dear Ms. Donovan:

As an elected member of the City Council of the City of Cathedral City, I request
written confirmation of the telephone advice given by your staff member, John
Wallace, on April 24, 1990, concerning use of surplus campaign funds.

As I advised Mr. Wallace, in the recently concluded election, my campaign
committee raised funds greater than the expenditures made for the election. I
propose to use a part of those funds to further my service as a City Council
Member by obtaining training in the use of computers at a local educational
establishment.

The City of Cathedral City is now, and has for some time been, a very "computer
literate" City with computer terminals available to virtually all staff members;
all members of the City's executive staff (department heads) not only have
terminals at their desks, but also in their homes; in addition, the City has
assisted Councilmembers in obtaining a "home computer" (PC) linked to the City's
mainframe by telephone modem. In my particular case, I am also one of only two
designated officers or employees of the City authorized to have direct access
into the League of California Cities' state wide computer information and
bulletin board service "State Net".

The City of Cathedral City had, until very recently, provided training programs
in the utilization of computers, but because of other involvements, I was never
able to take such city provided training, and the City's Data Processing staff
is now so overloaded with work that they have ceased providing this training
program.

With adequate training, I feel that I will be able to utilize the computer
program access available from my home. I will be able to access virtually all
information on the City's mainframe, including budget information, spreadsheets,
memos on all kinds of city problems, and electronic mail, as well as utilize the
League of California Cities' comprehensive State Net computerized information
exchange program.

P.O. BOX 5001 • 68-625 PEREZ ROAD • CATHEDRAL CITY, CA 92235-5001 • 619 / 324-8388 • FAX: 619 / 324-4816

Ms. Katherine Donovan
April 24, 1990
Page 2


I am not otherwise employed, and have no purpose in learning computer utilization
except to improve the service I am able to render to city staff and to the
constituency which has elected me. I consider such training to be directly
related to the governmental purpose of enabling me to better serve my
constituents through access to virtually unlimited resources of information which
the City makes possible through its mainframe and through its State Net hook-
ups.

Since computer training is thus directly related to a government purpose, it is
my belief that use of surplus campaign funds to reimburse me for a computer
training program would be an appropriate use of such funds.

Following my discussion with John Wallace of your staff, and his discussions with
our City Attorney, Allen R. Briggs, Mr. Wallace has indicated that such use of
surplus campaign funds would be an appropriate use.

I request confirmation of Mr. Wallace's advice in this matter.

Your consideration in this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
RENA M. "PAT" MURPHY
Councilmember

PMM:tsc

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste 600
Sacto CA 95804-0807

April 26 1990          FPPC
                       Apr 30 10:06 AM '90

Dear Commission:

Please confirm that the group of Menlo Park residents that meet to support
candidates for a City Council election in November is not a PAC and does
not have to register with you, as it will have
     no dues
     no fund raising arm.
It's purpose will be to discuss issues, support those candidates that are
most in tune with the committee's goals, act as helpers to the campaign
committee of these candidates, but not ask for funds nor accept funds in the
name of the group. Each candidate will have his/her own campaign manager
and will report funds on an individual basis, and if people offer money to the
committee, the response will be, please send the check to Candidate X at
such and such an address.

I have the booklet Campaigning in California, and I think we are not expected
to register, as we will not be a money raising committee, but I would like
your answer to this so that others who ask, and are confused about the issue
may rest assured that we will not be a Political Action Committee as
defined in section Recipient Committee on page 3.

Thank you,

Mrs Isabel Sewell
1049 Noel Dr * 1
Menlo Park CA 94025


[illegible]
